misconceptionSummary: Students can sometimes simplify mechanically but do not understand why only like terms can be combined.

visuals.ruleText: Like terms must have the same variable part
visuals.subText: You can add 3x and 2x because they are the same kind of quantity

steps.narration: Collecting like terms works because the terms represent the same kind of quantity.
Three x and two x are both x terms, so they can be combined. But three x and two y are different kinds of terms, so they cannot be collected together.
You can think of three x as three copies of x and two x as two more copies of x. Altogether that makes five copies of x, which is five x.
This also matters in context. In a perimeter expression, x terms collect with x terms and constants collect with constants.

visuals.expression: Collect only terms that match in variable and power
visuals.label: Same variable part means same kind of algebraic quantity

steps.narration: The key idea is matching structure. Terms can only be combined when their variable part is the same.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: combining terms because they both contain letters, for example 2x + 3y = 5xy. Different variable parts mean different kinds of terms.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Only like terms combine. Same letter is not enough if the whole variable part is different.
